chapter_title.en: Chapter 123 – The Tawaaf and touching the corners

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/25)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/25)
text: كُنْتُ مَعَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَطُوفُ فَكَانَ لَا يَمُرُّ فِي طَوَافٍ مِنْ طَوَافِهِ بِالرُّكْنِ الْيَمَانِيِّ إِلَّا اسْتَلَمَهُ ثُمَّ يَقُولُ اللَّهُمَّ تُبْ عَلَيَّ حَتَّى أَتُوبَ وَ اعْصِمْنِي حَتَّى لَا أَعُودَ .

en.hubeali: A number from our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Al Numan, from Ibrahim Bin Sinan, from Abu Maryam who said,

‘I was with Abu Ja’farasws circumambulating, and it was such that heasws did not pass by the Yamani corner during hisasws Tawaaf except that he touched it, then heasws was saying: ‘O Allahazwj! Turn towards measws (with Mercy) until Iasws repent, and Protect measws until Iasws do not repeat’.
en.sarwar: 14. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from al-Hassan ibn al-Nu’man from Ibrahim ibn Sinan from abu Maryam who has said the following: “I once was performing Tawaf with abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and in every Tawaf on reaching al- Yemaniy corner he would touch it and say, ‘O Lord, forgive and turn to me so I repent and protect me so I will not repeat.’” NOTE:This Hadith is best explained through the Fatwas.
